Language.name: English
Language.String:
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Home City Shipments are earned slightly faster. Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Pikeman (Tericos), Rodelero (Espadachins), Lancer (Garrochistas) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
War Dog, Missionary, Rodelero, Lancer, Gatling Gun and Gaucho
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Lombard 

The succesor to the Viceroyalty of the Rio de la Plata, following the end of its long civil war in 1861 Argentina enjoyed a relative era of peace for the remainder of the 19th Century, and experienced a large influx of European Immigrants, particularly Italians.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Can train Privateers with increased build limit, and which are allowed to train several units.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Barbary Warrior, Corsair Marksman, Tribal Horseman, Bedouin Horse Archers, Muhbir and Great Bombard

From the late Middle Ages on, Piracy was rampant in the Western Medditerranian based in a number of North African states. In 1805 US Marines led sucessful action against Tripoli, which initiated the decline of the Barbary States in the 19th Century.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Receives a free Covered Wagon with every Age and starts with 7 Citizens, which cost 70 coin. Town Centers automatically spawn Voluntarios Da Patria, which are weaker but faster Revolutionaries.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Musketeer (Legionarios), Dragoon (Legion Dragoons), Crossbowman (Order Besteiros), Pikeman (Ordinance Pikemen) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Volunterio Da Patria and Gatling Gun

Portugal begain settling what is now Brazil in the 16th Century. In 1822, Brazil declared independence from Portugal and established the Empire of Brazil, which lasted until 1889.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Cows gather more from Haciendas and you can have 3 times as many cows. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Industrial Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Chinaco (Charro), Salteador (Vigilante) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Padre, Insurgente, Salteador, Soldado, Chinaco, Californio
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

 On June 14th 1846, American immigrants declared indipendence from Mexico in San Fransisco. 25 days later, the rebels were absorbed into the California Battalion of the American Army.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Manor spawns a Metis Pathfinder when built. Villagers gain extra ranged attack with each Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Musketeer (Redcoats), Hussar (King's Life Guard) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Ranger and Congreve Rocket

 British Colonization of Newfoundland began in the 16th Century. With victory in the Seven Years War, Britain took control of French Canadian holdings. Canada remained loyal to the British during the American Indipendence War and was an important front in the War of 1812. In 1867 Britain granted greater autonomy to the Canadian Dominion.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Artillery can be trained early and Forts can be built in Commerce Age. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Commerce Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Chinaco (Charro), Salteador (Vigilante) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Padre, Insurgente, Salteador, Soldado, Chinaco
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

The Federal Republic of Central America first declared its indipendence from Spain in 1821 and then from the Mexican Empire in 1823. After their second civil war ended in 1840, the Federal Republic dissolved and the constituent States gained sovereignty.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Home City Shipments are earned slightly faster. Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Pikeman (Tericos), Rodelero (Espadachins), Lancer (Garrochistas), Hussar (Hussar of Death) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
War Dog, Missionary, Rodelero, Lancer, Gatling Gun

 Following the conquest of the Incas, Spanish colonization of Chile began in earnest, where they faced stiff resistance from the Mapuche and other native tribes. In 1810, after Napoleon's deposition of the Spanish monarchy, Chile established an autonomous loyalist government, but morphed into full indipendece by 1818. The century that followed included much conflict with its neighbors, as well as internal wars.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Home City Shipments are earned slightly faster. Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Musketeers are replaced with Indipendece Guards, which have a bonus against Cavalry at range, and with each Age gain additional attack and hitpoints but lose speed.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Pikeman (Tericos), Rodelero (Espadachins), Lancer (Garrochistas)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
War Dog, Missionary, Rodelero, Lancer, Gatling Gun

Spanish Conquistadors landed in Colombia early in the 16th Century in search of gold. Named "New Granada" it was initially incorporated into the Viceroyalty of Peru, before having its own Viceroyalty of New Granada established. Quickly it became known for its scientific minds in biology and astronomy. Colombia declared indipendence in 1810 but its Indipendence war would not conclude until 1822.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Khevite Fusiliers replace Revolutionaries, which can change their stats with their stance. Can train Mamelukes and Stradiot from the Stable, Forts, and Galleons, and receive 1 free Mameluke with each Shipment starting in Fortress Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Deli (Rumeliot) and Humbaraci (Ulufeli)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Azap, Janissary, Imam, Bashibozuk, Muhbir, Sipahi, Deli, Abus Gunner, Humbaraci, Great Bombard and Galley

In 1517 the Ottoman Empire subjegated Mamluke Egypt. After Napoleon's 1798 Egyptian campaign, an internal power struggle resulted in Muhammad Ali Pasha outsing the Mamlukes from power, and carved out defacto independence. The Ottomans declared Egypt a Khedivate, or autonomous vassal state, in 1867, and it was soon after that the Suez Canal was completed in partnership with France.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Can build up to 7 Saloons. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Commerce Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Chinaco (Charro), Salteador (Vigilante) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Padre, Insurgente, Salteador, Soldado, Chinaco, Filibuster
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

In 1853 William Walker led a company of Filibusters to capture La Paz and declared himself president of the new Republic of Baja California. After several months resistance by Mexican locals and armed forces, Walker surrendered himself to the US Navy and was charged with violating the Neutrality Act, but was acquitted by his jury.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Torps gather nearby resources. Advanced improvements are available in the Arsenal. Can train Karelian Jaegers in addition to Settlers, which can build some buildings and gather some resources. Can build up to 6 Taverns. Jaeger can always be trained from Taverns.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Pikeman (Dalkarl), Hakkapelit (Drabant) and Grenadier (Pavlov Grenadiers)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Carolean, Karelian Jaeger, and Hakkapelit
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Blockhouses are a combination of Barracks and Outpost.

Finland was drawn into Sweden's influence startung in the Middle Ages by crusade and Swedish settlement. Finnish soldiers distinguished themselves in the Thirty Years' War with their Hakkapelitta. Russia occupied Finland several times in the 18th Century, but it wasn't until 1809 that Sweden lost control of Finland, at which time it was incorporated into the Russian Empire as the semi-autonomous Grand Duchy of Finland.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Begins with a Native Scout. Trains Metis Voyageurs instead of Settlers. Villagers gain extra ranged attack with each Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Skirmisher (Voltigeurs), Cuirassier (Gendarmes)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Metis Voyageurs and Cuirassier

The settlement of what became New France began with Jacques Cartier's expedition to the Gulf of St. Lawrence in 1534. Eventually the territory stretched from Louisiana to Newfoundland. The main export of the territory was the fur trade, made possible by the relatively good relations between the native peoples and the French settlers. With France's defeat in the Seven Years' War it ceded its North American possesions to Britain and Spain, although French Canadian culture endured centuries on.
Sample

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Begins with a Native Scout. Town Centers and Taverns slowly spawn Pirates for free. Can train Privateers, which are allowed to train Pirates. Maroons replace Revolutionaries, which are cheaper and have more range and speed, but less hitpoints.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Maroon

The Island of Hispaniola was the site of Cristopher Columbus' first landing in the New World. Spain ceded the western half of the island to France, and the sugar plantations worked by slaves would make it France's most profitable colony. The slaves fought a revolution from 1791 to 1804 and sucessfully expelled the French. The next century was marked by much internal hardship and political instabilty.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Starting in the Commerce Age, receive an Outpost Wagon with every Home City Shipment. Outposts when built spawn a new unit in each Age: a Hajduk, a Grenadier, a Pandour, and a Hussar. Grenzers replace Revolutionaries, which have more range and can pick up treasures, but have less hitpoints. Hungarian Grenadiers replace Grenadiers, which have more range and can be trained from the Barracks and Outposts.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Hussar (Magyar Hussar)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Grenzer, Hungarian Grenadier and Galley

Descendents of the Magyars who fought Otto the Great, Hungary was a powerful state in the late Middle Ages and an important bulwark against Ottoman expansion into Europe, but was subsumed into the Habsburg patrimony in the 18th Century. Despite the nominal faliure of the 1848 revolution, this led the way for greater importance within the Habsburg Empire.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Can train Wokou Junks.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Halberdier (Stadswacht), Ruyter (Carabineer)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Ruyter, Javanese Spearmen, Cetbang Cannon and Fluyt

The Dutch East India Company was formed in 1602 for the purpose of controlling the spice trade in the region now known as Indonesia, but was nationalized in 1799 following its bankruptcy. Dutch hegemony of Indonesia was often tenous and varied from region to region, with a number of revolts across its colonial history culminating in the Aceh War at the end of the 19th Century.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Can train Holcan Javelineers and research Mayan technologies in the Native Embassy, and the build limit of Holcan increase with each Home City Shipment. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Imperial Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Salteador (Vigilante) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Native Villager, Padre, Yucateco Insurgente, Cruzob Avenger, Cruzob Infantry, Chaac Canoe
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

After the conquest of the Aztecs, the Spanish and their native allies turned towards the many Mayan states of the Yucatan. It took nearly 200 years for the Maya to be completely subjegated, and even afterwords Mayan culture persisted. During the chaotic second Yucatan Republic, many Mayan communities rose in revolt. From 1847 until the end of the century, the Maya achieved defacto independence centered around Chan Santa Cruz.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Home City Shipments are earned slightly faster. Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Gains Simon Bolivar upon reaching Fortress Age, who slightly boosts the hitpoints of nearby infantry and cavalry.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Pikeman (Tericos), Rodelero (Espadachins), Lancer (Garrochistas)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
War Dog, Missionary, Rodelero, Lancer, Gatling Gun

The Quechuan Inca forged an empire across the 15th Century in modern Peru, but were in turn conquered by the Spanish with native auxilleries in the 16th Century. The new Viceroyalty of Peru became an important source of mineral wealth for the Spanish Empire, but when the flow of bullion slowed in the 18th Century the reforms meant to address it only resulted in civil unrest, epitomized in the Tupac Rebellion. In the early 19th Century, with Spanish power waning as a consequence of Napoleon's invasion, South America erupted into a patchwork of independence wars, but Peru remained a loyalist stronghold. That is, until forces led by Simon Bolivar and Jose de San Martin, heroes to the Independent Colombia and Argentina respectively, brought their armies to bear against the divided loyalists and by the end of 1824 had soundly defeated Spanish forces.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Trains Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Manor spawns a Citizen when built. Forts spawn 2 Gatling Guns when built. Gains a General upon reaching Fortress Age, who can plant an Inspiring Flag.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Gatling Gun and Congreve Rocket

Starting in the 17th Century from small settlements like those of Jamestown and Plymouth, British of various backgrounds moved to the New World and organized themselves into 13 Colonies stretching the eastern coast of North America. Following the Seven Years War, the British Parliment levied additional taxes upon the colonists to try and recouperate its cost, without the assent of the colonies. These and other grievences convinced most of the American elite that Independence was prefferable, and with the eventual assistance of France and Spain fought and won that right.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Trains Coureurs instead of Settlers and keeps a stronger conventional economy from the opening seconds. Gains an Eclaireur (mounted scout) upon reaching Commerce Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Skirmisher (Voltigeurs), Cuirassier (Gendarmes)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Eclaireur and Cuirassier

Napoleon Bonaparte rose from the chaos of the French Revolutionary Wars to dominate the French state as First Consul and then Emperor. His regime preserved many of the Revolution's military reforms, but redirected France away from radical republican upheaval and toward a centralized imperial war machine built on discipline, artillery, and mass mobilization.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Trains Sans Culottes instead of Settlers, which are stronger in combat but gather more slowly. Gains an Eclaireur (mounted scout) upon reaching Commerce Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Skirmisher (Voltigeurs), Cuirassier (Gendarmes)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Sans Culottes, Eclaireur and Cuirassier

In 1789 with debt and social discontent mounting, Louis XVI convoked the Estates General soliciting advice from his subjects. However, the Third Estate, representing the commoners but helmed by the Mercantile class and New Rich, boldly siezed control and repurposed it into the National Assembly in a bid for sovereignty. Guided by Enlightenment thinkers such as Voltaire and Rousseau, the revolutionaries targeted first the Nobility, then the Clergy, and finally the Monarchy. The new French Republic, fueled by a zealous revolutionary mania, sought to remake the whole of French society, and its Committee enacted a reign of terror upon the citizens of Paris, as well as revolutionary wars of expansion, seeking to spread their ideals by sword and cannon. In 1799, the charismatic general Napoleon Bonaparte siezed control of the fracturous government, first as Consul and then Emperor of the French, and continued his war against all Europe for another 15 years. This quarter century of near constant bloodshed left an indelable mark upon Europe and shaped the course of world events for centuries to come.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Home City Shipments are earned slightly faster. Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Can train Mexican Outlaws from the Saloon, in addition to local Outlaws.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Pikeman (Tericos), Rodelero (Espadachins), Chinaco (Charro)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
War Dog, Missionary, Rodelero, Chinaco, Cuerudo

Spanish explorers had only discovered the east coast of Mexico in 1518, but by 1521 rouge conquistador Hernan Cortez had conquered the Aztec Empire with the help of locals subjegated by the Aztecs. Based in Mexico city, this colony dubbed the Viceroyalty of New Spain slowly expanded from California to Panama over the next two centuries, along the way fighting various native tribes as well as rival European powers. With Napoleon's invasion of Spain in 1808, Mexican elites siezed the moment to argue for home rule. The next 70 years were marked by political instability, civil war, and revolution.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Can research Guard level improvements in Fortress Age but take somewhat longer to research before Industrial Age. May train Mortars, Horse Artillery, and Heavy Cannons an age early. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Fortress Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Chinaco (Charro), Salteador (Vigilante) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Padre, Insurgente, Salteador, Soldado, Chinaco
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

With the settlement of the 1824 Constitution, Mexico was made a Federal Republic. In the 1830's, President Santa Anna centralized the Mexican state under himself, suspended the Mexican Constitution, and abolished the Congress. Resistance to the central government coalesed in northern Mexico along the Rio Grande, and in 1840 the Republic of the Rio Grande was declared. Covertly supported by the Republic of Texas, the Rio Grande army won several victories against Santa Anna's forces but ultimately were reabsorbed into Mexico by 1841.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Train Citizens instead of Settlers, which cost 70 coin. Starting in the Commerce Age, receive an Outpost Wagon with every Home City Shipment. Outposts when built spawn a Rosior Dragoon, which have more speed and attack but less hitpoints than a normal Dragoon. Dorabants replace Revolutionaries, which have more hitpoints but less range and are more expensive.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Dorabant, Rosior Dragoon and Galley

Following the Mongol yoke, the Romanian principalities of Wallacia, Moldavia, and Transylvania fell under Ottoman hegemony. The three joined the Holy League against the Ottomans in 1594, and were briefly united under Micheal the Brave. Transylvania was incorporated into the Habsburg domain in the 17th century, but not without difficulty. The Ottomans sent Phanariotes to oversee Moldavia and Wallachia but the ensuing instability allowed the Habsburgs and Russians to take more territory. Taking inspiration from the Greek Revolution, revolt arose first in 1821 and again in 1848, but Romania would not be fully independent until 1877.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Villagers and Banks gather faster. Receives a free Trek Wagon with every Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Halberdier (Stadswacht), Ruyter (Carabineer)
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Ruyter, Trek Wagon and Fluyt
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Banks to automatically produce coin

Portugese explorer Bartolomeu Dias would be the first to round the Cape of Good Hope in 1487, but it wasn't until the 1650's that Dutch settlers would begin to arive, establishing Cape Town as a supply stop for the Dutch East India Company between Europe and Asia. The British would occupy Cape Town in 1795 and again in 1806 to deny it from the French, who had themselves occupied the Low Countries at this time. With the Congress of Vienna, South Africa was ceded permanently to the British Empire, and British Settlers began arriving in earnest. Seeking to escape the British colonial administration, many descendents from Dutch Settlers, known as Boers, began to migrate by wagon to interior South Africa, now called the Great Trek. The Boers would come into conflict with the Xhosa, Zulu, and by the end of the 19th Century the British themselves.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Minutemen levies can be called repeatedly from Town Centers and Outposts. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Industrial Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Chinaco (Charro), State Militia (Volunteer) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Padre, State Militia, Regular, Sharpshooter, Minuteman, Chinaco, Gatling Gun
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

Spain long considered Texas part of its territory, but only seriously enforced it when the French constructed Fort Saint Louis. With Mexican independence from Spain, Texas became part of Mexico. Many Americans immigrated to Texas at this time, but when Santa Anna sought to centralize the Mexican government under himself, the new Texans thought this a tyrany. They rose in rebellion and declared independence in 1836 as the Texas Republic. The Texans had ongoing conflicts with the Mexicans and Comanche for the next decade, before being annexed by the United States in 1845.

<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Civilization bonus:</color>
Insurgentes are improved to Yucateco Insurgentes, which gain a Lasso charged attack. Gain two bonus cards upon reaching Fortress Age.
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>ELITE UNITS - NEVER ROUT:</color>
Chinaco (Charro), Salteador (Vigilante) 
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique units:</color>
Padre, Yucateco Insurgente, Salteador, Soldado, Chinaco
<color=1.0, 1.0, 0.0>Unique buildings:</color>
Can build Haciendas and a Cathedral which may research unique improvements 

The Yucatan peninsula's remoteness from Mexico City and challenging geography long made it difficult to control by the Viceroyalty of New Spain. With Mexican Indipendence War beginning in 1810, the local Yucatan elite proposed their own republic. The Yucatan was incorporated into Mexico peacefully in 1824, as the new constitution aligned with their own federal ideals, but with the presidency of Santa Anna the Mexican state became much more centralized. Thus in 1840 the Republic of the Yucatan declared its Indipendence from Mexico, however after 8 years of sporradic conflict they would be reunited.
Imperial Revolutionaries
Imperial upgrade for Revolutionaries.
Chilean Hussar attack and hitpoints greatly increased.
Imperial Death Hussar
Heavy Cetbang
Cetbang attack and hitpoints increased.
Imperial Cetbang
Imperial upgrade for Cetbang.
Imperial Javanese Spearmen
Imperial upgrade for Javanese Spearmen.
Imperial Javanese Spearman
Hungarian Hussar attack and hitpoints greatly increased.
Imperial Magyar Hussar
Veteran Hungarian Grenadier
Guard Hungarian Grenadier
Imperial Hungarian Grenadier
Imperial Barbary Warriors
Imperial upgrade for Barbary Warriors.
Imperial Tribal Horsemen
Imperial upgrade for Tribal Horsemen.
Imperial Tribal Horseman
Imperial Bedouin Horse Archers
Imperial upgrade for Bedouin Horse Archers.
Imperial Bedouin Horse Archer
Egyptian Mamelukes
You receive a free Mameluke with all your shipments, starting with this one.
Imperial Californios
Imperial upgrade for Californios.
Imperial Picket
Imperial Independence Dragoon
Imperial Fencible
Imperial Texian Lancer
Notably increases the melee damage of Cavalry Archers and Halberdier. Cavalry Archers inflict area damage in melee.
Veteran Llaneros
Veteran upgrade for Llaneros.
Guard Llaneros
Guard upgrade for Llaneros.
Imperial Llaneros
Imperial upgrade for Llaneros.
Veteran Jagunços
Veteran upgrade for Jagunços.
Guard Jagunços
Guard upgrade for Jagunços.
Imperial Jagunços
Imperial upgrade for Jagunços.
Veteran Granaderos
Veteran upgrade for Granaderos.
Guard Granaderos
Guard upgrade for Granaderos.
Imperial Granaderos
Imperial upgrade for Granaderos.
Veteran Llanero
Guard Llanero
Imperial Llanero
Veteran Jagunço
Guard Jagunço
Imperial Jagunço
Veteran Granadero
Guard Granadero
Imperial Granadero
Observer: Water Window
Observer-only control. Fires the naval observation window early so docks, fishing, and fleet movement can be inspected without waiting for the normal timer.
Observer console: the water window was triggered manually from the Town Center.
Observer: Commander Window
Observer-only control. Fires the commander behavior window early so rout, elite support, elite retreat, and formation behavior can be inspected without playing the economy forward.
Observer console: the commander window was triggered manually from the Town Center.
Observer: Repeat Fight
Observer-only control. Grants the repeat-fight rebuild package early so the same engagement can be rerun after the first swing.
Observer console: the repeat-fight window was triggered manually from the Town Center.
Language.string: Forte de Santo Antonio de Gurupá
Forte de São Francisco Xavier de Piratininga
Casa da Torre de Garcia d'Ávila
Forte de Santa Cruz de Itamaracá
Forte de Santo Antônio da Barra
Forte de Santo Antônio Além do Carmo
Fortaleza de Santa Cruz da Barra
Fortaleza de São João da Barra do Rio de Janeiro
Forte de São Paulo da Gambôa
Forte de São João da Bertioga
José de Barros Falcão de Lacerda
San Carlos de la Barra Fortress
Castillo de Santa Rosa de la Eminencia
Fuerte de San Sebastián del Pastelillo
José Inácio de Abreu e Lima
Pierre Gaultier de Varennes, sieur de La Vérendrye
Étienne de Veniard, Sieur de Bourgmont
Louis de la Corne, Chevalier de la Corne
Pierre de Troyes, Chevalier de Troyes
Presidio de San Antonio de Béjar
Language.String: A New World
Flat 2v2 test arena for A New World AI behavior, including AI rout, elite support, elite retreat, and coordinated team fights.
Open west-vs-east Texas-style test field with three land lanes and two naval lanes for fast AI validation.
A New World Maps
Custom validation maps for A New World AI skirmish testing.
Revolutionary France
Napoleonic France
Napoleon Bonaparte
Maximilien Robespierre
Thomas Jefferson
Miguel Hidalgo
Isaac Brock
Louis-Joseph Papineau
Pedro I of Brazil
José de San Martín
Bernardo O'Higgins
Andrés de Santa Cruz
Simón Bolívar
Toussaint L'Ouverture
Prince Diponegoro
Paul Kruger
Carl Gustaf Mannerheim
Lajos Kossuth
Alexandru Ioan Cuza
Hayreddin Barbarossa
Muhammad Ali Pasha
Francisco Morazán
Juan Bautista Alvarado
Felipe Carrillo Puerto
Antonio Canales Rosillo
Jacinto Canek
Mariano Vallejo
Sam Houston
Napoleonic French
Revolutionary French
Americans
Mexicans
Canadians
French Canadians
Brazilians
Argentines
Chileans
Peruvians
Colombians
Haitians
Indonesians
South Africans
Finnish
Hungarians
Romanians
Barbary
Egyptians
Central Americans
Baja Californians
Yucatecans
Rio Grandeans
Mayans
Californians
Texians
Queen Elizabeth I
British · Tudor privateer navy + Manor mercantile (Queen Elizabeth I)
Muhammadu Kanta of Kebbi
Hausa · Sokoto Caliphate jihadist mobilization (Usman dan Fodio)
Ivan the Terrible
Russian · Oprichnik terror + Streltsy consolidation (Ivan the Terrible)
Maurice of Nassau
Dutch · mercantile drill + fortification (Maurice of Nassau)
Menelik II
Ethiopian · Adwa-victorious highland defense (Menelik II)
Shivaji Maharaj
Indian · Maratha hill-fort guerilla (Shivaji Bhonsle)
Montezuma II
Aztec · tribute empire (Montezuma)
Pachacuti
Inca · Andean terrace fortress (Pachacuti)
Isabella I of Castile
Spanish · conquistador crown (Isabella I)
Louis XVIII
French · Bourbon Ancien Régime · Restoration court and gendarmerie (Louis XVIII)
Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla
Mexican · Grito de Dolores clergy-led revolution (Hidalgo)
George Washington
American · continental republic (Washington)
Prince Henry the Navigator
Portuguese · maritime discovery (Prince Henry the Navigator)
Giuseppe Garibaldi
Italian · Risorgimento Redshirt levee (Garibaldi)
Gustavus Adolphus
Swedish · Hakkapelit cavalry + line infantry (Gustavus Adolphus)
Jean Parisot de Valette
Maltese · Hospitaller siege endurance (Jean de Valette)
Kangxi Emperor
Chinese · banner-army central planning (Kangxi Emperor)
Suleiman the Magnificent
Ottoman · Janissary siege modernization (Suleiman the Magnificent)
Tokugawa Ieyasu
Japanese · Sakoku consolidated shogunate (Tokugawa Ieyasu)
Hiawatha
Haudenosaunee · Confederacy council (Hiawatha)
Chief Gall
Lakota · Little Bighorn envelopment · Plains cavalry wedge (Chief Gall)
Frederick the Great
German · oblique-order Prussian drill (Frederick the Great)
A New World DLC
A New World DLC (%d)
Always outnumbered, never outfought. We march.
You are neither Austrian nor French. I shall have to improvise.
Bastions, sally ports, covered ways. Vauban's geometry, Prussian discipline.
Walls delay. They do not deny. Silesia still answers to Berlin.
A Hussar lost. His regiment continues without him.
Oblique order. Concentration at the flank. The Austrian army folds as designed.
För Gud och fäderneslandet. God and fatherland — we march together.
The Lion of the North is abroad. Kneel or face the Hakkapeliitta.
Stockholm's core holds. The Carolean redoubts ring the approach.
Walls? I brought cannon. Walls are a matter of patience.
A brave Swede lost. His widow will have the Crown's purse.
As at Breitenfeld — combined arms, light cannon, cavalry flank. The day is ours.
The six fires burn together. We speak with one voice.
You have not learned the Great Law. We will teach you.
The longhouse stands, the palisade rises. The village will be whole.
He hides as a weak brother would. The Confederacy does not hide.
A warrior has gone to the Great Peace. We sing his name tonight.
The circle closes. The Great Law is older than this war.
Stand with the Tsar. The Oprichnina does not forget loyalty.
Traitor — Moscow tolerates no rival. Your lands shall be ash.
The Kremlin's walls rise. Let them dash themselves upon stone.
He cowers. Even cowards must answer to the rod.
A faithful servant slain. His killers will be hunted to the steppes.
God's will. The earth bows to Moscow as it must.
A good day to ride together. Hoka hey.
You have come to the Paha Sapa. You will not leave.
The Lakota do not wall. The camp moves.
He builds walls. He has forgotten how to fight.
A brother rides the spirit trail. Today is a good day.
As at the Greasy Grass — the cavalry rode in, and did not ride out.
O Roma, o morte! With you, comrade, we make Italy.
I have fought kings and popes. You are no worse than either.
The Risorgimento holds its cities, block by block.
Another tyrant behind stone. The Redshirts will climb his wall.
A Redshirt fallen. Italy remembers her sons.
Mille cammie rosse — and one more victory for the nation.
The sea is wide and the horizons are ours. We sail together.
I have charted stranger shores than you. Your coast, too, will be known.
Sagres stands on the cliff. The harbor is walled, the sea is open.
He walls the land. I command the sea. The sea wins.
A sailor lost. The ocean takes and gives.
The caravel returns victorious. New shores, new victories.
¡Viva la Virgen de Guadalupe! Caminamos juntos.
¡El Grito de Dolores! Spain will hear us from the pulpit itself.
La Alhóndiga stands. The iglesias become fortresses when the Republic calls.
A gachupín hides. Our machetes do not tire.
Un hijo de Dolores ha caído. The Republic honors him.
¡Por la independencia! The parish rises, the viceroy's men fall.
For Crown and Cross, we ride as one. God wills it.
Granada fell. Then Iberia. Then the New World. Do not be the next line.
The alcázar holds. No Moor, no heretic shall pass.
Walls do not save those who oppose the true faith.
A hidalgo gave his life. He dies in grace.
As at the Reconquista — the Cross advances, the field is ours.
For the Order and for the Faith. We stand together.
The Turk came to Malta and left his bones. You shall not do better.
Birgu, Senglea, St. Elmo. Three rings of stone and the will of God.
Walls. The Hospitaller knows walls better than any man alive.
A knight falls into God's hand. We mourn and continue.
As at the Great Siege — the Turk broke, the Order held. Deo gratias.
The Son of Heaven greets his ally. The Mandate runs through both our hands.
The Qing do not address barbarians twice. Kneel, or depart.
The Great Wall was built for such moments. Stone and patience prevail.
Barbarian walls crumble before imperial cannon. It has ever been so.
A banner-servant is lost. His family is entered in the Imperial register.
The Eight Banners prevail, as the Mandate requires. Order is restored.
By drill and discipline, my friend. The Dutch way.
You have not studied the line. You will learn.
The polders flood, the walls stand. The Republic holds.
Even stone yields to persistence. Mark his weakness.
A good officer, gone. Send word to his city.
Volley and counter-march. They broke as drilled men expected.
Abyssinia greets her friends. We fight together for the highland.
Adwa is not yet finished. Come and see.
The amba is a wall by nature. The stone is only for the gate.
A foreigner building walls on our earth. Let him build his grave.
A son of the highland falls. We do not forget.
As at Adwa: the mountain does not bow. The field is ours.
The gods smile on this alliance. Their blood binds ours.
The smoking mirror shows me your end. Come, meet it.
The chinampas float. The causeways guard. Tenochtitlan endures.
He walls as if the jaguar could not climb. Foolish.
An offering. The sun drinks. We continue.
The flower war ends in victory. As the calendar foretold.
Soldiers! Forty centuries look down upon us. We march together.
Impossible is a word only found in the dictionary of fools.
Marshal, field fortifications only. The Grande Armée advances, not hides.
He builds walls. Excellent — his army will be delivered to us like pastry.
Un officier brave est tombé. We avenge him with French arms.
As at Austerlitz — surprise them on the flank, and the Empire takes another page.
Viracocha sees our alliance. The Four Suyus stand with you.
You are far from your sea. The mountain will teach you.
The stones of Sacsayhuamán fit without mortar. As the land itself.
A lowlander building walls in the highland. The altitude is his enemy before I am.
A son of Inti falls. The ancestors remember.
As the Inca hand built Cusco: patient, stone by stone. Victory follows.
We hold these truths self-evident. And we hold this line together.
The tree of liberty must be refreshed from time to time. Prepare to water it.
The frontier blockhouse. Wood, powder, and the will of free men.
Tyranny behind stone. A republic will find the cracks.
A yeoman fallen. His farm shall be honored.
As at Yorktown — the citizen-soldier prevails when the cause is just.
Seamos libres, lo demás no importa. Avanzamos juntos.
He cruzado los Andes. Un campo de batalla no me detendrá.
Los fortines de la frontera. No ciudadelas — solo vigías.
Se esconde. El gaucho no teme paredes.
Un patriota de la Pampa. Los Andes lo recuerdan.
Como en Chacabuco: los Andes en la espalda, la victoria en el puño.
Desde Alta California hasta Baja. Amigos, adelante.
La Californias no se entregan sin la palabra de sus gobernadores.
El presidio de Loreto. Adobe y torre. La misión es fortaleza.
Amurallado como un gringo. Poca imaginación.
Un californio caído. Las misiones lo recuerdan.
El pronunciamiento triunfa. California se defiende.
The sea is my cavalry and the sun my banner. We sail together.
Algiers has no fear of empires. Ask Charles V.
The Kasbah, the Peñón. Sea-facing guns, land-side mur. The harbor endures.
Walls on the land? I sail around them.
A corsair lost to the sea. Allah gathers.
The Crescent on the mast, the ports of the Mediterranean in our wake.
Independência ou morte! Marchamos juntos, amigo.
O Brasil é um império novo. Você descobrirá sua força.
A fortaleza do Rio guarda a baía. A costa é nossa.
Paredes? A marinha imperial não teme pedras.
Um súdito leal caído. O Império o honra.
Independência. Brasil é livre, e a vitória é nossa.
Desde Sonoma hasta Monterey. Amigo, la tierra nos une.
La Californias tiene sus propios gobernadores. Tú no eres uno.
El Presidio de Sonoma. Adobe y bastión, como los españoles enseñaron.
Un gringo que se esconde. Ni ranchero ni californio.
Un californio leal. El rancho honra su nombre.
El pronunciamiento se mantiene. California se gobierna sola.
For King and the Canadas, we stand together.
The Americans came to Detroit and left with bowed heads. You, too?
Fort Wellington, Fort Henry. The frontier blockhouse holds.
A Yankee behind walls. We know this posture.
A loyal servant of the Crown. His family will be kept.
As at Queenston Heights — the militia and the redcoat together. The Canadas hold.
Por la Federación Centroamericana. Unidos prosperamos.
Los conservadores han aprendido. Tú también aprenderás.
Los presidios coloniales. Adobe y palisada. La federación guarda.
Se esconde como el godo colonial. Lo conocemos.
Un federalista caído. La Patria Centroamericana lo honra.
La Federación resiste. La causa liberal avanza.
¡Por la patria! Vivir con honor o morir con gloria.
La cordillera nos separa. Pero no por mucho tiempo.
El paso del Andes se guarda. Valparaíso es nuestra puerta.
Paredes contra los que cruzan los Andes? Absurdo.
Un huaso valiente caído. El Ejército Libertador lo honra.
Como en Maipú: Chile libre, y el campo nuestro.
Por la Gran Colombia, por la libertad de un continente. Marchamos juntos.
He liberado cinco naciones. La suya será la sexta.
Los pasos andinos. Boyacá enseñó que la montaña defiende la libertad.
El tirano tras pared. Bolívar los ha cruzado todas.
Un patriota americano. Los Andes lo recuerdan para siempre.
Como en Carabobo: el llanero, el criollo, el libertador. Victoria americana.
The Nile welcomes an ally. Egypt rises again under our hand.
Mamluks learned fear at my table. You are not a Mamluk.
The Citadel of Cairo holds. The delta villages palisaded.
The Nizami Cedid will test his walls. Ottoman method, Egyptian arm.
A faithful Egyptian lost. The Nile remembers.
Egypt modernizes. The old Ottoman way bends to new cannon. Victory.
Suomi seisoo. Finland stands with you, comrade.
The forest, the snow, and the sisu. You will meet all three.
The Mannerheim Line. Trenches, forests, no stone. The terrain is the wall.
Stone walls on Finnish soil. Foreign concepts.
A ranger fallen in the snow. The forest remembers.
Finnish sisu prevails. As always, against weather and enemy both.
Pour la patrie canadienne-française. Ensemble, camarade.
Vous avez oublié la résistance québécoise? Nous la rappellerons.
La seigneurie tient. Les palissades et les Patriotes.
Il se cache derrière la pierre. Les Patriotes connaissent les bois.
Un Patriote tombé. Son nom sera gravé.
Vive le Québec libre! Le drapeau des Patriotes flotte encore.
Liberty or the grave. We walk together, mon ami.
I overthrew an empire with enslaved hands. Yours is no harder task.
The Citadelle rises on the mountain. The French shall not return.
A white master behind walls. The ancestors laugh.
Un citoyen noir tombé. Liberté est écrite avec son sang.
As at Crête-à-Pierrot — the free men hold, the empire falls.
Éljen a magyar! Barátom, a szabadság köt minket.
Habsburg-ally or worse? Magyarország does not yield again.
The Military Frontier palisade. Forward settlers, forward defense.
He hides. The Honvéd ride past walls.
Egy hős elesett. Hungary carves his name.
The hussar wedge strikes home. Magyar steel, as ever.
The Java War continues in our time. With you, saudara.
The VOC has been here before. They do not stay.
Bamboo palisade. The kraton holds, the forest does the rest.
Dutch walls. We know these and their seams.
Seorang prajurit yang jatuh. Allah akan menerimanya.
As in the Java War — the mountain, the forest, the guerrilla. Victory of the free.
Los cenotes nos unen. Hermano, hermana maya, caminamos.
Tu emperador es como el sol — cae cada noche.
Chan Santa Cruz. La selva es la pared. El cenote es la entrada.
Paredes contra la selva? Absurdo español.
Un maya caído. Los dioses recogen.
La selva oculta, el cenote alimenta, el maya resiste. Siempre.
¡Viva México! Con la Virgen de Guadalupe caminamos juntos.
¡Por el Grito de Dolores! You shall hear it from every parish.
The Alhóndiga holds. The church bells rally the faithful.
El gachupín se esconde. The machetes do not tire.
Un hijo de Dolores ha caído. La Virgen lo recibe.
¡Independencia! One more viceroy learns to flee.
Grande Armée! Let the eagles fly with yours.
Kings have learned my name through their retreats. You will too.
Marshal, temporary fieldworks only. We march at dawn.
He walls. Bring up the Imperial Guard and the artillery park.
An aide-de-camp lost. He dies for the Emperor.
Austerlitz repeats itself whenever the Grande Armée appears. Continue.
La Confederación está firme. Juntos, amigo.
Perú y Bolivia son uno. No sabes qué has despertado.
Los pasos andinos se fortifican. La altitud hace el resto.
Se esconde en el llano. El cóndor sobrevuela.
Un hijo del Altiplano. Inti lo recibe.
La Confederación vence. La historia andina sigue escribiéndose.
Liberté, Égalité, Fraternité! Citoyen, we advance.
The tyrant's head fell in Paris. Yours is not so different.
Barricades in the rue. The Republic holds the city, and the city is all.
Ancien régime walls. They fell before. They fall again.
Un citoyen martyr. The Committee will carve his name.
Virtue and terror have carried the day. The Republic is one step closer to the ideal.
La República del Río Grande, amigo. Libertad del norte.
Mexico y los gringos querían ambos. Ninguno nos tuvo.
El presidio de Laredo. Adobe, poblados fronterizos, milicia.
Se amuralla en territorio nuestro. Qué atrevimiento.
Un republicano caído. El norte lo recuerda.
La República resiste. Del Nueces al Sabinas, es nuestra.
Prietene, de la Dunăre la Crișana, suntem uniți.
Unirea Principatelor este făcută. You come too late to divide us.
The Carpathian pass palisade. The mountain guards, the palisade closes.
Ziduri? Dar domnia Moldovei a văzut multe turci cădea.
Un fiu al Moldovei a căzut. Patria îl ține.
Ca la unirea — Moldova și Valahia împreună, biruitoare.
The Lord is with Transvaal. And with you, broer.
The Boer does not yield. Majuba reminded the Empire. We shall remind you.
Laager. The wagon circle. Not walls, but will.
Khaki behind stone. The veld knows how to wait.
'n Boer gesneuwel. God sien hom.
As at Majuba — the farmer shoots true, the Empire retreats. Transvaal holds.
Remember the Alamo. And stand with us today.
San Jacinto was eighteen minutes. I have time to spare.
The mission fortress. Alamo, Goliad, Gonzales. We know this ground.
A Mexican behind walls? Santa Anna tried that too.
A Texian gone. Another name for the wall of heroes.
As at San Jacinto — Santa Anna fled, Texas stood. The Republic endures.
Henequén y libertad. Caminamos juntos, compañero.
La Casta War enseñó a los hacendados. Tú aprenderás lo mismo.
Mérida tras sus muros coloniales. Las haciendas fortificadas.
Se esconde. El cenote siempre encuentra la grieta.
Un yucateco caído. El pueblo no olvida.
Henequén, tierra, y victoria. El Yucatán socialista prevalece.
Hindavi Swarajya! The hills and I rise with you.
I am the jackal of the Sahyadri. You will see only my shadow.
The fort on the peak. The ridge holds. Let them come uphill.
Walls on the plain. He has not read his Shastras.
A Maratha falls. The monsoon washes. We strike again tomorrow.
Struck, melted, returned. The Mughal way is not our way.
By the grace of Allah, we greet our ally. The sword of the Padishah is at your side.
I am Suleiman, Sultan of Sultans. Kneel, or I shall teach you.
The walls of Constantinople endure. As they have since Theodosius.
Walls. My bombards at Rhodes answered walls. Yours will answer, too.
A Janissary falls. Paradise receives him.
As Mohács — the Sultan's shadow sweeps the field. The Crescent rises.
The river wears the stone. Patience binds us.
Sekigahara decided the matter. You come late, and from the wrong flank.
Edo castle holds. The moat is deep and the sakoku is eternal.
Walls do not stop the patient river. The stone will wear.
A samurai finds his honor. We continue, as he would wish.
Victory not by the quickest blade, but by the patient one. As always.
The sword of the faithful rides with yours. Peace upon you.
The Caliphate of Sokoto calls all unbelievers to account.
The mud-brick holds. Kano's walls are ancient, and they hold.
Walls will not save a kingdom that has lost its righteousness.
A faithful martyr. Allah gathers his own.
The horse of Sokoto rides first, and last. The field is ours.
The cause of liberty binds us both. Steady now.
We fight for independence. You will find that an unshakable thing.
Valley Forge taught us: dig in, endure, and the spring brings victory.
Let him sit behind walls. The Continental Army has time.
A patriot fallen. His family will be cared for by the Republic.
As at Yorktown — patience, line of supply, final stroke. Liberty's logic.
Stand fast with us. England's purse and powder are at thy side.
Pretender — thou shalt find our sea-dogs at thy throat ere long.
Our walls are raised; our cannon primed. Let them dare.
He cowers behind stone. We shall starve him of trade and of hope.
A loyal servant is fallen. England remembers her own.
As we foretold — God blew, and they were scattered. Long live England.
French Empire
French Republic
American Republic
Mexico
Canada
Lower Canada
Brazil
Argentina
Chile
Peru
Gran Colombia
Haiti
Indonesia
South Africa
Finland
Hungary
Romania
Barbary States
Egypt
Central America
Baja California
Yucatán
Rio Grande
Maya
California
Texas
Britain
France
Netherlands
Spain
Portugal
Germany
Russia
Ottoman Empire
Aztec Empire
Iroquois Confederacy
Japan
China
Mughal India
Inca Empire
Sweden
Ethiopia
Sokoto
Mexico
Italy
Malta

The Aztec Empire dominated Mesoamerica through military conquest and sophisticated urban planning. Even after Spanish conquest, Aztec descendants preserved their cultural identity and military traditions.

Aggressive early game with strong Eagle Warriors; focus on building strong defensive perimeter with unique units.

Key units: War Dog, Eagle Warrior, Jaguar Warrior, Puma Spearman
Key bonus: Priests can build shrines to generate experience; strong eagle warrior line
Age up: Eagle warrior shipments and native alliance cards

Queen Elizabeth I's England established naval dominance and expanded colonial holdings globally through merchant ventures and trading posts. Britain pioneered industrial advancement and built a maritime empire.

Economy-focused with strong navy advantage. Manor-spawned settlers enable aggressive booming. Wall harbor early, then push naval and Musketeer lines.

Key units: Longbowman, Congreve Rocket, Ranger, Musketeer
Key bonus: Manors spawn Settlers automatically; strong shipping and naval economy
Age up: Wagon politicians (Trading Post, Bank, Manor shipments)

The Kangxi Emperor presided over the Qing Dynasty's expansion and prosperity, establishing China as a dominant continental and maritime power. Confucian traditions guided governance and military affairs.

Defensive, village-based economy with strong gunpowder units. Use multiple town centers and cohesive unit formations for disciplined warfare.

Key units: Qiang Pikeman, Chu Ko Nu, Mongol Horse Archer, Fire Lance Cavalry
Key bonus: Multiple towncenters for economic advantage; monastery generates XP
Age up: Imperial age shipments and technology research

Maurice of Nassau revolutionized military tactics and led the Dutch Revolt against Spanish dominance. Amsterdam became the center of a mercantile empire controlling global trade routes.

Economic power through trade and banking. Strong cavalry and merchant fleet; focus on sea control and defensive fortifications.

Key units: Ruyter, Halberdier, Skirmisher, Fluyt
Key bonus: Banks and merchants generate gold passively; strong traders and navy
Age up: Trade-focused shipments and banking cards

Menelik II defeated Italian colonial forces at Adwa, securing Ethiopian independence and modernizing the kingdom. Ethiopia maintained its sovereignty while other African nations fell to European colonization.

Strong elite units and defensive play. Limited but high-quality military with focus on well-coordinated formations.

Key units: Shotel Warrior, Archers, War Elephant, Mounted Archer
Key bonus: Elite warriors with bonus defensive stats; can build unique religious structures
Age up: War elephant and elite warrior shipments

Louis XVIII restored the Bourbon monarchy after Napoleon's fall, stabilizing France through cautious diplomacy. France remained a cultural and diplomatic powerhouse despite post-Napoleonic instability.

Elegant and flexible military with strong cavalry. Effective at both offense and defense through balanced unit composition.

Key units: Cuirassier, Dragoon, Pikeman, Musketeers
Key bonus: Home City Shipments arrive faster; cavalry units more effective
Age up: Cuirassier and wagon politician shipments

Frederick the Great transformed Prussia into a military superpower through disciplined tactics and artillery innovation. German military culture emphasized precision, formation discipline, and rapid mobilization.

Mass production of disciplined line infantry and artillery. Effective at breaking enemy lines through concentrated firepower and formation tactics.

Key units: Uhlan, Skirmisher, Doppelsoldner, Artillery, Grenadier
Key bonus: Mass-produce cheap line infantry; Guard upgrades available earlier
Age up: Line infantry and artillery shipments

Hiawatha unified the Six Nations Confederacy, establishing a sophisticated governance system based on consensus and mutual defense. The Haudenosaunee maintained their independence through strategic alliances and superior tactics.

Mobile and aggressive with strong scouting and raiding capabilities. Build through settlements and alliances with other native tribes.

Key units: Tomahawk, Travois, War Canoe, Iroquois Warrior
Key bonus: Native allies provide bonuses; settlements and lodges generate experience
Age up: Native alliance and warrior shipments

Muhammadu Kanta expanded the Kebbi kingdom through military prowess and established dominance over Hausa lands. Hausa cities became centers of Islamic learning and trans-Saharan trade.

Cavalry-focused warfare using superior mounted archers and warriors. Control territory through fast-moving mounted units and defensive fortifications.

Key units: Cavalry Archer, War Elephant, Musketeers, Royal Guard
Key bonus: Cavalry units are faster and more effective; trade routes generate gold
Age up: War elephant and cavalry shipments

Pachacuti transformed the Inca Empire into the largest pre-Columbian state in the Americas through military conquest and administrative innovation. Terrace farming and road networks unified vast mountain territories.

Strong economy through tribute and terraced farming. Build extensively but defend carefully; specialized units for mountain warfare.

Key units: Inca Warrior, War Chief, Slinger, Llama Herds
Key bonus: Terraces provide food; road network enables faster movement
Age up: War chief and warrior shipments

Shivaji Maharaj founded the Maratha Empire, resisting Mughal dominance and establishing Hindu sovereignty. His guerrilla tactics and naval power challenged the largest empire in India.

Versatile and adaptive military strategy. Strong in both open field and defensive play with effective cavalry and elephant units.

Key units: Horseman, Spearman, War Elephant, Cannon
Key bonus: Villagers gather resources faster; elephants are strong assault units
Age up: War elephant and cavalry shipments

Garibaldi unified Italy through military campaigns and popular revolution, ending centuries of fragmentation. Italy emerged as a modern nation-state with growing industrial and naval power.

Balanced military with strong artillery and cavalry. Italian discipline and tactical flexibility enable both aggressive and defensive strategies.

Key units: Horseman, Skirmisher, Cannon, Infantry
Key bonus: Artillery and cavalry receive combat bonuses; defensive structures stronger
Age up: Artillery and cavalry shipments

Tokugawa Ieyasu established the Tokugawa Shogunate, bringing peace and stability to feudal Japan. Bushido culture and military discipline defined Japanese society for centuries.

Disciplined and honorable warfare with strong melee units and archers. Excel at defensive play and controlling terrain through fortifications.

Key units: Samurai, Ashigaru Musketeer, Naginata Rider, War Monk
Key bonus: Samurai and melee units extremely strong; temples generate XP
Age up: Samurai and honor-based shipments

Gall led the Lakota people during the era of Great Plains warfare and cultural resistance. The Lakota became renowned for their exceptional horsemanship and warrior traditions.

Lightning-fast cavalry raids with strong scouting and harassment. Effective at hit-and-run tactics and controlling open plains terrain.

Key units: Mounted Archer, Travois, Dog Soldier, Sioux Commando
Key bonus: Mounted units faster and harder to hit; hunting for extra food
Age up: War horse and cavalry shipments

Jean de Valette defended Malta against Ottoman siege, establishing the island as a Christian stronghold in the Mediterranean. Malta became a center of naval power and religious authority.

Strong defensive and naval play with excellent fortifications. Control the sea and defend positions; strong mercenary and naval shipments.

Key units: Knight, Heavy Cavalry, Crossbowman, Galley
Key bonus: Fortifications stronger; mercenary access enhanced
Age up: Knight and galley shipments

Hidalgo launched the Mexican Independence War, sparking revolutionary fervor across Latin America. Mexico emerged as an independent nation with rich cultural traditions and strategic territory.

Flexible and revolutionary with strong priest and mounted units. Haciendas provide economic advantage; cavalry and mercenaries form the backbone.

Key units: Padre, Chinaco, Soldado, Vaquero
Key bonus: Haciendas generate extra resources; cathedral for unique improvements
Age up: Revolutionary and cavalry shipments

Suleiman the Magnificent ruled the Ottoman Empire at its apex, dominating three continents through military innovation and administrative genius. Ottoman culture blended Islamic, Persian, and Byzantine influences.

Powerful gunpowder-focused military with excellent siege capability. Janissaries and Abus Gunners provide firepower; flexible and adaptable.

Key units: Janissary, Abus Gunner, Sipahi, Deli
Key bonus: Gunpowder units strong; mosques generate XP and research bonuses
Age up: Janissary and gunpowder shipments

Henry the Navigator pioneered global exploration, establishing Portuguese dominance of oceanic trade routes and African commerce. Portugal built the first truly global maritime empire.

Naval-focused with strong mercantile advantage and early gunpowder units. Control sea lanes and establish trading posts for economic dominance.

Key units: Caravel, Musketeer, Pikeman, Cassador
Key bonus: Naval units strong; trading posts provide economic advantage
Age up: Naval and trading post shipments

Ivan the Terrible expanded Moscow into a vast empire, establishing Russia as a major European power. Russian military strength and vast territory defined the emerging superpower.

Powerful and resilient with strong mounted archers and streltsy. Economic resilience through Cossacks and vast resources; defensive depth.

Key units: Streltsy, Cossack, Mounted Archer, Heavy Cannon
Key bonus: Siege powerful buildings effectively; cavalry and archers strong
Age up: Cossack and streltsy shipments

Isabella of Castile sponsored Columbus's voyage and unified Spain through the Reconquista. Spain became a global superpower through conquest and exploration in the New World.

Conquistador-focused with strong priests and explorers. Build missions for influence; use crossbowmen and pikemen for disciplined formations.

Key units: Conquistador, Pikeman, Crossbowman, Cannon
Key bonus: Missionaries convert natives; conquistadors very strong
Age up: Conquistador and missionary shipments

Gustavus Adolphus revolutionized military science and made Sweden a great power through disciplined tactics and innovation. Swedish military dominance shaped 17th-century European conflicts.

Disciplined and innovative military tactics with mobile artillery and strong cavalry. Effective at breaking lines through concentrated force.

Key units: Uhlan, Musketeer, Pikeman, Field Cannon
Key bonus: Artillery very mobile and accurate; cavalry discipline and damage high
Age up: Artillery and cavalry shipments

George Washington led the American Revolution against British rule, establishing the United States as an independent nation dedicated to liberty and democratic principles.

Militiamen and minutemen provide flexible defense and rapid mobilization. Strong irregular warfare and defensive capabilities with economic resilience.

Key units: Minuteman, Regular, Sharpshooter, Field Artillery
Key bonus: Militia rapidly rebuilt; strong defensive economy
Age up: Militia and independence shipments

Thomas Jefferson expanded America westward and established democratic ideals. Post-revolutionary America pursued expansion, technological innovation, and industrial development.

Industrial and expansionist with strong settlers and economic output. Build settlements and railroads; mechanized army focused on artillery.

Key units: Regular, Sharpshooter, Field Artillery, Rifleman
Key bonus: Settlers fast; industrial bonuses for production
Age up: Industrial and expansion shipments

José de San Martín liberated Argentina and neighboring territories from Spanish colonial rule. Argentina emerged as a prosperous nation with vast agricultural lands and European immigration.

Agricultural focus with strong cavalry and revolvers. Use open plains for cavalry dominance; economic advantage through estancias.

Key units: Gaucho, Rodelero, Lancer, War Dog
Key bonus: Cavalry and gauchos very strong; estancia bonuses
Age up: Cavalry and gaucho shipments

Manuel Pineda Muñoz led Baja California's resistance against filibuster invasions and internal disorder. The region struggled for stability while maintaining its distinct identity.

Flexible and adaptable with strong settlers and ranching economy. Use saloons and mercenaries; cavalry-focused military.

Key units: Vaquero, Soldado, Ranchero, Padre
Key bonus: Saloons enable mercenary training; ranching economy
Age up: Ranching and mercenary shipments

Hayreddin Barbarossa and his successors built a pirate empire dominating Mediterranean trade. Barbary States challenged European naval supremacy for centuries.

Naval corsairs and pirates with strong privateers. Control sea lanes and raid merchant shipping; naval warfare and privateer economy.

Key units: Corsair Marksman, Tribal Horseman, Barbary Warrior, Galley
Key bonus: Privateers raid shipping; naval units very strong
Age up: Corsair and pirate shipments

Pedro I declared Brazilian independence from Portugal and established a stable empire. Brazil developed as the largest and most economically powerful nation in South America.

Strong economy through plantations and African units. Flexible military with revolutionaries and strong artillery.

Key units: Voluntario da Patria, Lancer, Rodelero, War Dog
Key bonus: Plantations and slavery economy; strong unit multiplier
Age up: Agricultural and military shipments

Mariano Guadalupe Vallejo led California through Mexican independence and American conquest. Californios became legendary horsemen and frontier leaders.

Cavalry-dominated with vaqueros and rancheros. Use open terrain for mounted advantage; saloons and ranching economy.

Key units: Vaquero, Ranchero, Soldado, Californio
Key bonus: Vaqueros extremely fast; ranching economy strong
Age up: Cavalry and ranching shipments

Isaac Brock defended Canada against American invasion, earning military renown. Canada developed as a frontier power balancing British loyalty and continental independence.

Frontier defense with strong villagers and local alliances. Trappers and voyageurs provide economic advantage; ranged units effective.

Key units: Ranger, Trapper, Voyageur, Musketeer
Key bonus: Trappers generate furs; frontier defense strong
Age up: Frontier and ranger shipments

Francisco Morazán championed Central American federation and independence from Spanish colonial rule. The region struggled with fragmentation while maintaining distinct identity.

Flexible and adaptive with strong artillery and defense. Use terrain and fortifications effectively.

Key units: Soldado, Chinaco, Padre, Field Cannon
Key bonus: Defensive structures strong; artillery effective
Age up: Military and artillery shipments

Bernardo O'Higgins led Chilean independence and established a stable republic. Chile developed as a disciplined military power with strong institutions.

Disciplined military with strong cavalry and infantry. Effective at both offensive and defensive play through tactical excellence.

Key units: Lancer, Rodelero, Pikeman, Hussar
Key bonus: Cavalry discipline exceptional; military training fast
Age up: Cavalry and military shipments

Simón Bolívar liberated Colombia and surrounding territories, envisioning Gran Colombia. Colombia became the center of Bolívar's revolutionary empire in South America.

Revolutionary and aggressive with strong mounted units. Use cavalry for rapid strikes and mountain terrain for defense.

Key units: Lancer, Rodelero, War Dog, Missionary
Key bonus: Cavalry strong; revolutionary units effective
Age up: Revolutionary and cavalry shipments

Muhammad Ali Pasha established Egyptian independence within the Ottoman sphere, modernizing the nation and establishing a dynasty. Egypt became the most powerful Arab state.

Strong military through mamluke traditions and modernization. Cavalry and mamelukes form elite striking force.

Key units: Mameluke, Khevite Fusilier, Stradiot, Sipahi
Key bonus: Mamelukes very strong; military training enhanced
Age up: Mameluke and cavalry shipments

Carl Gustaf Emil Mannerheim defended Finnish independence against Soviet invasion. Finland emerged as a proud Nordic nation maintaining its sovereignty through military strength.

Defensive and disciplined with strong blockhouses and jaegers. Forest and snowy terrain favor Finnish tactics.

Key units: Karelian Jaeger, Carolean, Hakkapelit, Blockhouse
Key bonus: Blockhouses combine barracks and outpost; jaeger training fast
Age up: Jaeger and defensive shipments

Louis-Joseph Papineau championed French-Canadian identity and autonomy within British North America. French Canadian culture persisted and thrived despite English dominance.

Voyageur-focused economy with strong coureurs and mounted units. Use forests and waterways effectively.

Key units: Coureur, Voyageur, Musketeer, Cuirassier
Key bonus: Voyageurs gather resources and move fast; fur trade economy
Age up: Voyageur and trapper shipments

Toussaint Louverture led the Haitian Revolution, establishing the first Black republic. Haiti's independence shocked the colonial world and inspired liberation movements.

Revolutionary and defiant with strong maroons and pirates. Irregular warfare and naval attacks; guerrilla tactics effective.

Key units: Maroon, Privateer, Pirate, Musketeer
Key bonus: Maroons and pirates strong; irregular warfare effective
Age up: Revolutionary and pirate shipments

Lajos Kossuth led the Hungarian Revolution against Habsburg dominance, establishing Hungarian nationalism. Hungary emerged seeking greater autonomy and cultural recognition.

Aristocratic and cavalry-focused with strong hussars and grenadiers. Use cavalry charges and well-coordinated assaults.

Key units: Hussar, Grenadier, Grenzer, Pandour
Key bonus: Hussar very strong; noble cavalry traditions provide bonuses
Age up: Hussar and grenadier shipments

Diponegoro led Indonesian resistance against Dutch colonialism, becoming a legendary military hero. Indonesia fought for independence and cultural preservation.

Jungle-focused with strong irregular units and naval strength. Use terrain advantage and localized uprisings.

Key units: Native Fighter, War Elephant, Cetbang, Canoe
Key bonus: Jungle units effective; local uprising capabilities strong
Age up: Resistance and jungle warrior shipments

Jacinto Canek led Mayan resistance during the Caste War, establishing an independent Maya state. Mayans maintained their culture and independence in the Yucatan.

Native alliance-focused with strong holcan and ceremonial units. Use cenotes and native advantages.

Key units: Holcan, Native Villager, Yucateco Insurgente, Chaac Canoe
Key bonus: Native embassy provides bonuses; holcan javelineers strong
Age up: Native alliance and warrior shipments

Benito Juárez led Mexico's liberal reforms and defended against French intervention, establishing secular republic. Mexico emerged with renewed national pride.

Revolutionary with strong insurgentes and priests. Use rural uprisings and defensive fortifications.

Key units: Insurgente, Chinaco, Padre, Soldado
Key bonus: Rural uprising capabilities; priest conversion effective
Age up: Revolutionary and insurgent shipments

Napoleon Bonaparte dominated Europe through military genius and imperial ambition. Napoleonic warfare revolutionized military tactics and reshaped European boundaries.

Aggressive and dominating with elite cuirassiers and coordinated assaults. Napoleon's strategic brilliance translates to powerful military maneuvers.

Key units: Cuirassier, Gendarme, Voltigeur, Horse Artillery
Key bonus: Elite cavalry devastating; coordinated assaults extremely powerful
Age up: Elite cavalry and artillery shipments

Andrés de Santa Cruz united Peru and Bolivia briefly before dissolution, establishing a powerful Andean confederation. Peru emerged as a key South American power.

Andean warrior traditions with strong mountain units and cavalry. Effective in high-altitude combat.

Key units: Rodelero, Lancer, War Dog, Missionary
Key bonus: Mountain warfare effective; cavalry strong
Age up: Cavalry and mountain warrior shipments

Maximilien Robespierre embodied the French Revolution's radical phase, executing The Terror and attempting to remake French society. Revolutionary fervor spread across Europe.

Revolutionary and zealous with sans-culottes and popular uprising. Use mass mobilization and passionate infantry.

Key units: Sans Culottes, Voltigeur, Cuirassier, Field Cannon
Key bonus: Revolutionary units strong; popular uprising rapid
Age up: Revolutionary and citizen shipments

Antonio Canales Rosillo led the Rio Grande Republic's brief independence from Mexico. The region's unique culture and strategic location made it a contested frontier.

Frontier raiding with strong cavalry and mercenaries. Use terrain advantages and fast-moving units.

Key units: Chinaco, Vaquero, Soldado, Padre
Key bonus: Cavalry fast and effective; frontier economy
Age up: Cavalry and frontier shipments

Alexandru Ioan Cuza unified Wallachia and Moldavia, establishing modern Romania and pursuing reform. Romania emerged as a Balkan power seeking independence from Ottoman control.

Balanced military with strong cavalry and dragoons. Effective at defensive positions and mounted assaults.

Key units: Dragoon, Rosior, Dorabant, Pandour
Key bonus: Dragoon and cavalry effective; outpost units strong
Age up: Cavalry and dragoon shipments

Paul Kruger led Boer resistance against British colonialism, fighting to preserve Afrikaner independence. South Africa emerged from colonial conflicts seeking self-determination.

Resilient and adaptive with strong cavalry and settler economy. Use trek wagons and fast-moving mounted units.

Key units: Trek Wagon, Cavalry, Rifleman, Outpost
Key bonus: Trek wagon generates resources; defensive economy strong
Age up: Settler and cavalry shipments

Sam Houston led Texas independence from Mexico and negotiated annexation into the United States. Texas became a frontier power balancing independence and integration.

Frontier militia with strong minutemen and mercenaries. Rapid defense and irregular warfare effective.

Key units: Minuteman, Regular, Mercenary, Militia
Key bonus: Militia rapidly rebuilt; frontier defense strong
Age up: Militia and frontier shipments

Felipe Carrillo Puerto championed Yucatan independence and indigenous rights. The Yucatan pursued autonomy while maintaining cultural traditions and Maya heritage.

Maya-focused with indigenous units and defensive strength. Use cenotes and local uprisings for advantage.

Key units: Yucateco Insurgente, Cruzob Avenger, Padre, Chaac Canoe
Key bonus: Indigenous uprising powerful; religious influence strong
Age up: Indigenous warrior and uprising shipments
